name: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN)
description: An initiative by the government of India in which all farmers will get up to ₹6,000 per year as minimum income support.
eligibility: All landholding farmers' families, which have cultivable landholding in their names.
benefits: ₹6,000 per year payable in three equal installments of ₹2,000 each.
application_process: Register on the PM-KISAN portal or through local Patwari/Revenue Officer.

name: Post Matric Scholarship for SC/ST/OBC Students
description: Financial assistance to students studying at post matriculation or post-secondary stage to enable them to complete their education.
eligibility: Students belonging to SC/ST/OBC categories studying in Class 11 and above. Must be a resident of the respective state.
benefits: Maintenance allowance, reimbursement of compulsory non-refundable fees, study tour charges, thesis typing/printing charges for Research Scholars.
application_process: Apply online through the State Scholarship Portal or National Scholarship Portal (NSP).

name: Pradhan Mantri Mudra Yojana (PMMY)
description: A scheme to provide loans up to ₹10 lakh to the non-corporate, non-farm small/micro enterprises.
eligibility: Any Indian Citizen who has a business plan for a non-farm sector income generating activity such as manufacturing, processing, trading or service sector.
benefits: Loans classified into three categories: Shishu (up to ₹50,000), Kishore (₹50,000 to ₹5 lakh), Tarun (₹5 lakh to ₹10 lakh).
application_process: Apply at any Bank, NBFC, or Micro Finance Institution.

name: Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB-PMJAY)
description: The world's largest health insurance scheme providing a health cover of Rs. 5 lakhs per family per year for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization.
eligibility: Poor, deprived rural families and identified occupational category of urban workers' families as per the latest Socio-Economic Caste Census (SECC) data.
benefits: Health cover of ₹5,00,000 per family per year for inpatient secondary and tertiary care.
application_process: Check eligibility online and get the Golden Card at empaneled hospitals or Common Service Centers (CSCs).

name: Stand Up India Scheme
description: Facilitates bank loans between 10 lakh and 1 Crore to at least one SC/ST borrower and at least one woman borrower per bank branch for setting up a greenfield enterprise.
eligibility: SC/ST and/or woman entrepreneurs, above 18 years of age. The enterprise must be a greenfield (first time) venture.
benefits: Composite loan (inclusive of term loan and working capital) between ₹10 lakh and ₹100 lakh.
application_process: Apply directly at a bank branch, through SIDBI's Stand-Up India portal, or via Lead District Manager.

name: Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana (SSY)
description: A government-backed savings scheme targeted at the parents of girl children, encouraging them to build a fund for the future education and marriage of their female child.
eligibility: Parents or legal guardians of a girl child up to 10 years of age.
benefits: High interest rate (subject to quarterly revision) and tax benefits under Section 80C. Maturity after 21 years.
application_process: Open an account at any post office or authorized commercial bank.

name: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Gramin (PMAY-G)
description: Aims to provide a pucca house with basic amenities to all houseless households and those living in kutcha and dilapidated houses in rural areas.
eligibility: Houseless families or those living in zero, one, or two-room kutcha houses in rural areas, as per SECC 2011 data.
benefits: Financial assistance of ₹1.20 lakh in plain areas and ₹1.30 lakh in hilly/difficult areas for house construction.
application_process: Beneficiaries are selected by Gram Sabhas based on SECC data. Registration is done through the AwaasApp or local Panchayat.

name: Atal Pension Yojana (APY)
description: A pension scheme primarily focused on the unorganized sector workers, providing a guaranteed minimum pension.
eligibility: All Indian citizens between 18 and 40 years of age who have a savings bank account.
benefits: Guaranteed minimum monthly pension ranging from ₹1,000 to ₹5,000 after attaining 60 years of age, depending on contributions.
application_process: Apply through any bank or post office where you hold a savings account.

name: National Means-cum-Merit Scholarship (NMMS)
description: Awards scholarships to meritorious students of economically weaker sections to arrest their drop out at class VIII.
eligibility: Students studying in class VIII who have secured minimum 55% marks in class VII. Parental income should not exceed ₹3,50,000 per annum.
benefits: Scholarship of ₹12,000 per annum (₹1000 per month) from Class IX to XII.
application_process: Apply online through the National Scholarship Portal (NSP) and pass the selection test conducted by State Govts.

name: PM Vishwakarma Yojana
description: A central sector scheme to support traditional artisans and craftspeople of rural and urban India.
eligibility: An artisan or craftsperson working with hands and tools in one of the 18 family-based traditional trades (e.g., Carpenter, Blacksmith, Goldsmith). Minimum age 18 years.
benefits: PM Vishwakarma Certificate & ID card, Skill Upgradation training, Toolkit Incentive of ₹15,000, Collateral-free Credit Support up to ₹3 lakh.
application_process: Enrollment through Common Service Centres (CSCs) and validation by Gram Panchayat and District Implementation Committee.

name: Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (PM-JAY)
description: The world's largest health insurance scheme fully financed by the government, providing a cover of Rs. 5 lakhs per family per year for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization.
eligibility: Poor and vulnerable families based on Socio-Economic Caste Census (SECC) database.
benefits: Cashless and paperless access to services at empanelled hospitals up to Rs. 5 lakhs.
application_process: Check eligibility online or at a CSC, and generate e-card at empanelled hospitals.

name: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana (PMAY)
description: An initiative to provide affordable housing for all in urban and rural areas.
eligibility: Economically Weaker Section (EWS), Low Income Group (LIG) and Middle Income Group (MIG) families who do not own a pucca house.
benefits: Credit linked subsidy on home loans, financial assistance for house construction/enhancement.
application_process: Apply online through PMAY portal or via Common Service Centres (CSC).

name: Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY)
description: A scheme to provide clean cooking fuel to poor households by distributing deposit-free LPG connections to women from BPL families.
eligibility: Adult woman belonging to a BPL family, with no existing LPG connection in the household.
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs. 1600 per connection, plus free first refill and hotplate.
application_process: Apply by filling the form at the nearest LPG distributor.

name: Atal Pension Yojana (APY)
description: A pension scheme for citizens of India focused on the unorganized sector workers, guaranteeing a minimum monthly pension.
eligibility: Any citizen of India between 18-40 years of age with a savings bank account.
benefits: Guaranteed minimum monthly pension ranging from Rs. 1000 to Rs. 5000 after the age of 60 years.
application_process: Apply through the bank or post office where the savings account is held.

name: Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana (SSY)
description: A government-backed savings scheme targeted at the parents of girl children, to build a fund for her future education and marriage expenses.
eligibility: Parents or legal guardians of a girl child below 10 years of age.
benefits: High interest rate, tax benefits under section 80C, and a maturity period of 21 years.
required_documents: Birth Certificate of the girl child
application_process: Open an account at any post office or authorized commercial bank.

name: Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA)
description: A scheme that guarantees 100 days of wage employment in a financial year to a rural household whose adult members volunteer to do unskilled manual work.
eligibility: Any rural household whose adult members are willing to do unskilled manual work.
benefits: Guaranteed 100 days of employment; unemployment allowance if work is not provided within 15 days.
application_process: Register at the local Gram Panchayat to obtain a Job Card.

name: Stand-Up India Scheme
description: Facilitates bank loans between 10 lakh and 1 Crore to at least one Scheduled Caste (SC) or Scheduled Tribe (ST) borrower and at least one woman borrower per bank branch for setting up a greenfield enterprise.
eligibility: SC/ST and/or women entrepreneurs, above 18 years of age.
benefits: Bank loans from Rs. 10 Lakh to Rs. 1 Crore for setting up new enterprises in manufacturing, services, or trading sector.
application_process: Apply through the Stand-Up India portal, through the lead district manager, or directly at a bank branch.
